examples: Early Christians in Europe adopted many of the practices of the older, pagan religions.
Some scholars believe that Yahweh was originally a tribal deity—a god whom the Hebrews worshiped and considered superior to the pagan gods.
And a pagan was elected to the New York City Council.
The solstice is a historically pagan celebration to mark the longest and shortest days of the year.
Druidry is to become the first pagan practice to be given official recognition as a religion.
